briefing: SoftBank plans to launch a robotics company focused on data centers, targeting a $100B IPO.
Microsoft increases its 2026 AI budget by $25 billion to address rising component costs.
Anthropic is reportedly seeking a new funding round of $50B, aiming for a $900B valuation.

articles.title: Microsoft levels up Azure Local to make it fit for large-scale sovereign clouds

articles.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO

articles.snippet: imbalanced-losses is a PyTorch library of training losses for class-imbalanced classification — including Focal Loss, Smooth-AP, and Recall-at-Quantile — with built-in DDP all-gather support for globally-correct rank estimation and normalization across multi-…
articles.ai_summary: The imbalanced-losses 0.2.3 library for PyTorch provides training losses tailored for class-imbalanced classification tasks, featuring built-in support for distributed data parallelism. This tool aims to improve model training in challenging scenarios.
articles.why_it_matters: Addresses a critical challenge in AI training, enhancing model performance in imbalanced datasets.

articles.title: Commentary: China's chip-model strategy pressures Nvidia's AI economics

articles.title: Samsung Restructures TV, Home Appliances Business Amid China Exit
articles.snippet: Samsung Electronics is restructuring its TV and home appliances business, weighing a China exit and closing plants in Malaysia and Slovakia, as VD and DA divisions swung to a 200 billion won loss last year.
articles.ai_summary: Samsung Electronics is restructuring its TV and home appliances divisions and considering exiting the Chinese market due to significant financial losses. This move reflects broader challenges in the global electronics market.

articles.title: Meta reportedly to cancel Manus acquisition as China clamps down on 'Singapore washing' in AI
articles.snippet: After China's National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) banned Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, the social media giant is reportedly preparing to unwind the deal and allow the founding team to exit in compliance with the cancellation. Original …

articles.title: OpenAI Crawl Activity Tripled Since GPT-5, Data Shows via @sejournal, @MattGSouthern
articles.snippet:
OpenAI crawl activity roughly tripled after GPT-5, with OAI-SearchBot now generating more log events than GPTBot.

articles.ai_summary: OpenAI's crawling activity has tripled since the launch of GPT-5, with its OAI-SearchBot generating more log events than its previous bot. This increase indicates heightened engagement and usage of OpenAI's technology.
articles.why_it_matters: Demonstrates growing interest and reliance on OpenAI's models in the tech community.

articles.title: Why is GitHub shifting Copilot billing? #tech
articles.snippet: GitHub is moving GitHub Copilot to usage based billing starting June 1, shifting from a model where users get premium requests bundled into subscriptions. The reported change introduces “monthly GitHub AI Credits,” tying cost to actual usage. The context matt…
articles.ai_summary: GitHub is shifting to usage-based billing for Copilot, starting June 1, moving away from subscription bundles to a model based on actual usage. This change aims to align costs with user activity.
articles.why_it_matters: Adjusts the economic model for AI tools, impacting developer budgeting and usage.

articles.title: DSO: Direct Steering Optimization for Bias Mitigation
articles.snippet: Generative models are often deployed to make decisions on behalf of users, such as vision-language models (VLMs) identifying which person in a room is a doctor to help visually impaired individuals. Yet, VLM decisions are influenced by the perceived demographic attributes of people in the input, which can lead to biased outcomes like failing to identify women as doctors. Moreover, when reducing bias leads to performance loss, users may have varying needs for balancing bias mitigation with overal
articles.ai_summary: Generative models, like vision-language models, can exhibit bias based on perceived demographic attributes, potentially leading to misidentification in critical scenarios. This raises concerns about balancing bias mitigation with performance, especially for users with varying needs.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the challenge of ensuring fairness in AI decision-making.
